task_completion.description: Open the original image in an image editing application
task_completion.visual_evidence: The original landscape photo (or a blue image with text 'EXPAND CANVAS') is visible within the workspace of a GUI image editor like GIMP.
A dialog box specifically for resizing the canvas (not scaling the image) is open in the image editor.

task_completion.description: Set the new canvas dimensions to 1000x800
task_completion.visual_evidence: The width is set to 1000 and the height is set to 800 in the canvas resize dialog.

task_completion.description: Center the original image content within the new canvas
task_completion.visual_evidence: The offset values in the dialog are set to center the image (e.g., X: 100, Y: 100), or the image is visually centered with equal added border space on all sides in the editor workspace.

task_completion.description: Save or export the expanded image
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save/export dialog is visible, or there is a notification/status bar indication that the file has been saved (e.g., as 'expanded_canvas.jpg').

integrity.description: The agent used a graphical image editor to perform the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a GUI application (like GIMP) rather than using terminal commands (like ImageMagick) to resize the canvas.

integrity.description: The agent modified the provided image file
integrity.visual_evidence: The image being edited is the original landscape photo (or the specific fallback image), not a newly created blank canvas.

integrity.description: The agent did not use scripts to bypass manual GUI interaction
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows showing Python scripts or shell commands being executed to perform the image manipulation.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Original image dimensions are 800x600 pixels. Target canvas dimensions are exactly 1000x800 pixels (±5 pixels tolerance). To keep the original content centered, the required offset is 100 pixels horizontally and 100 pixels vertically. The original file is typically located at '/home/ga/Desktop/landscape_canvas.jpg' and is an Unsplash landscape photo (or a blue synthetic image with text if download failed). The expected output file is typically saved as 'expanded_canvas.jpg' on the Desktop.
